back_translation: And Moses went and spoke these words to all Israel.
And he said to them: A son of a hundred and twenty years am I today; I am no longer able to go out and to come in, and the Lord has said to me: You shall not cross this Jordan.
The Lord your God, He is the one crossing before you; He will destroy these nations from before you, and you shall possess them. Joshua, he is the one crossing before you, as the Lord has spoken.
And the Lord will do to them as He did to Sihon and to Og, kings of the Amorite, and to their land, when He destroyed them.
And the Lord will give them before you, and you shall do to them according to all the commandment that I have commanded you.
Be strong and courageous; do not fear and do not tremble before them, for the Lord your God, He is the one going with you; He will not let you go and He will not forsake you.

And Moses called to Joshua and said to him before the eyes of all Israel: Be strong and courageous, for you will go with this people to the land that the Lord swore to their fathers to give them, and you shall cause them to inherit it.
And the Lord, He is the one going before you; He will be with you; He will not let you go and He will not forsake you. You shall not fear and you shall not be dismayed.
And Moses wrote this law and gave it to the priests, the sons of Levi, who carry the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and to all the elders of Israel.
And Moses commanded them, saying: At the end of seven years, in the appointed time of the year of release, in the Feast of Tabernacles,
when all Israel comes to appear before the face of the Lord your God in the place that He will choose, you shall read this law before all Israel in their ears.

Assemble the people, the men and the women and the little ones and your foreigner who is in your gates, so that they may listen and so that they may learn and fear the Lord your God, and they shall keep to do all the words of this law.
And their sons who did not know shall hear and learn to fear the Lord your God all the days that you are alive upon the ground that you are crossing the Jordan there to possess it.

And the Lord said to Moses: Behold, your days have drawn near to die. Call Joshua, and present yourselves at the tent of meeting, and I will command him. And Moses and Joshua went and presented themselves at the tent of meeting.
And the Lord appeared at the tent in a pillar of cloud, and the pillar of cloud stood at the entrance of the tent.

And the Lord said to Moses: Behold, you are about to lie with your fathers, and this people will rise up and play the harlot after the foreign gods of the land where they are coming into the midst of it, and they will forsake Me and break My covenant that I have cut with them.

And My anger will burn against him on that day, and I will forsake them and hide My face from them, and they shall be for consuming, and many evils and troubles shall find him; and he will say in that day: Is it not because my God is not in my midst that these evils have found me?
And I, hiding I will hide My face on that day because of all the evil that he has done, for he turned to other gods.
And now, write for yourselves this song and teach it to the sons of Israel; put it in their mouths, so that this song may be for Me as a witness in the sons of Israel.

When I bring him to the ground that I swore to his fathers, flowing with milk and honey, and he shall eat and be satisfied and grow fat, and he will turn to other gods and serve them, and they will reject Me and break My covenant.

And it shall be, when many evils and troubles find him, this song shall answer before him as a witness, for it shall not be forgotten from the mouth of his seed, for I know his inclination that he is doing today, before I bring him to the land that I swore.
And Moses wrote this song on that day, and he taught it to the sons of Israel.
And He commanded Joshua son of Nun and said: Be strong and courageous, for you will bring the sons of Israel to the land that I swore to them, and I will be with you.
And it was, when Moses finished writing the words of this law on a book, until their completion,
Moses commanded the Levites, those who carry the ark of the covenant of the Lord, saying:
Take this book of the law and place it beside the ark of the covenant of the Lord your God, and it shall be there in you as a witness.
For I know your rebellion and your stiff neck. Behold, while I am still alive with you today, rebellious you have been with the Lord, and how much more after my death!
Assemble to me all the elders of your tribes and your officers, that I may speak in their ears these words, and call as witnesses against them the heavens and the earth.

For I know that after my death, corrupting you will corrupt yourselves, and you will turn aside from the way that I have commanded you, and the evil shall meet you in the latter days, when you do what is evil in the eyes of the Lord, to provoke Him with the work of your hands.
And Moses spoke in the ears of all the assembly of Israel the words of this song until their completion.
